trials.supervisor_analysis: Baseline established at 1.34ms (PyTorch) with profiling showing GroupNorm=54%, Conv2d=30%, MaxPool=15%, Clamp<1%. Current Triton implementation scores 50.0 (correct but slower at 13.7ms). Advancing to optimization stage.

nudges.text: You've spent significant time trying to hand-write Triton GroupNorm/MaxPool kernels with correctness issues. Try `torch.compile` as your first optimization lever — create a `ModelNew` that wraps the reference model's forward with `torch.compile(mode="max-autotune")` and see if that beats the baseline. If you need a Triton kernel, focus on fusing only the simpler post-GroupNorm ops (scale * x, maxpool, clamp) into one kernel while keeping conv2d and group_norm as PyTorch calls.
